rulings.Case Title: STAN SCHULDNER VS. ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Tax Costs And For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.5
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF STAN SCHULDNER's Motion To Tax Costs And For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.5. Plaintiff Stan Schuldner's Motion to Tax Costs and for Sanctions under CCP 128.5 is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted Adn For Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, Line 11. CROSS COMPLAINANT VIOLIE D. PASTOR, AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE VIOLIE D. PASTOR REVOCABLE TRUST's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Cross-complainant Violie D. Pastor's unopposed Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Deem Matters Admitted Adn For Sanctions is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court orders as follows: (1) Cross-Defendant TA & TO HOLDINGS, L.P. has waived any objections to the Requests For Admission, set one, Form Interrogatories, set one and Inspection Demand, set one; (2) the Requests For Admission, set one, ##1-35 are deemed admitted; (3) Within 15 days of notice of this order, Cross-Defendant TA & TO HOLDINGS, L.P. must provide complete and straightforward, verified original responses to the Form Interrogatories, set one and Inspection Demand, set one, free of objections; (4) Within 15 days of notice of this order, Cross-Defendant TA & TO HOLDINGS, L.P. shall produce the requested documents or true copies thereof; and (5) Within 15 days of notice of this order, Cross-Defendant TA & TO HOLDINGS, L.P. and H. Douglas Daniel, and the Law Offices of H. Douglas Daniel, jointly and severally, shall pay Cross-complainant Violie D. Pastor the amount of $1,200 as sanctions, the formers' actions in connection with this discovery not being substantially justified. rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY LEE, ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, VS. PANDA EXPRESS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, Line 12. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY LEE's Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action. Plaintiff Jeffrey Lee's Unopposed Motion For Dismissal Of Representative Action is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The motion is infirm in several respects. First, Plaintiff did not submit proof of service on LWDA. Second, the motion itself requests dismissal with prejudice of all representative claims, whereas the Lu Declaration seeks dismissal with prejudice of some representative claims and dismissal without prejudice of others. Third, absent is a declaration under penalty of perjury identifying the similarities and differences between the claims in the instant action and those in Minas v. Panda Express Inc. L.A.S.C. 22STCV077753, and explaining with some particularity why dismissal of the representative claims in this action is appropriate. Fourth, the motion does not explain why Plaintiff seeks dismissal instead of, for example, leave to amend to limit the claims in this action to those not covered in the Minas action. The court understands that this action includes claims, even if identical, that cover a period earlier than the period covered by Minas. The court is concerned that if those claims are dismissed, even without prejudice, they will be forfeited entirely because a subsequent action would be untimely. Any party making a substantially similar motion in this case in the future shall include the text of this ruling in its papers. Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order that includes the above text. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MYHOA NGUYEN VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 20, 2025, Line 3 DEFENDANTs LLOYD DAMON, MATTHEW OERTLI, SHAGUN ARORA, FEIFEI XUE, NICOLE APPELLE, MATTHEW ALDRICH, MIGUEL CEREJO, UCSF HEALTH ACO, LLC, UCSF HEALTH COMMUNITY HOSPITALS, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, and UCSF MEDICAL FOUNDATION's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The following named defendants have specially appeared to move to quash service: (1) Lloyd Damon; (2) Matthew Oertli; (3) Shagun Arora; (4) Feifei Xue; (5) Nicole Appelle; (6) Matthew Aldrich; (7) Miguel Cerejo; (8) The Regents of the University of California; (9) UCSF Health ACO, LLC; (10) UCSF Health Community Hospitals; and (11) UCSF Medical Foundation (hereinafter, collectively, the "Specially Appearing Parties"). Specially Appearing Parties' unopposed motion to quash is GRANTED. The record demonstrates Plaintiff Myhoa Nguyen has not effectuated service on any of the Specially Appearing Parties and, thus, the court is currently without jurisdiction over any of them for purposes of this action. (See CCP section 418.10(a)(1).) The proofs of service filed on January 13 and 16, 2025, are quashed and of no effect. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/JMQ)
